body:
Title: CRA Form T2209 2025 Federal Foreign Tax Credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2209/t2209-25e.pdf

CRA T2209 2025 calculates federal foreign tax credits for line 40500. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.48 and 0.55. For the federal non-business foreign tax credit, line 1 is non-business income tax paid to a foreign country. Line 2 multiplies net foreign non-business income divided by net income by basic federal tax. Line 3 is the lesser of line 1 and line 2. Note 3 defines net income as line 23600 plus the amount on line 68360 of Form T1206, minus the listed deductions: Canadian Forces personnel and police deduction line 24400, security options deductions line 24900, other payments deduction line 25000, net capital losses of other years line 25300, capital gains deduction for qualifying business transfer or qualifying cooperative conversion line 25395, capital gains deduction line 25400, and listed line 25600 amounts. Note 4 defines basic federal tax for the non-business credit as line 42900 plus the federal dividend tax credit and any federal surtax on income allocated outside Canada, minus refundable Quebec abatement and federal refundable First Nations abatement.

For the federal business foreign tax credit, line 4 is business income tax paid to a foreign country for the year plus unused foreign tax credits for that country for the allowed years. Note 5 says that if the taxpayer was a resident of Quebec, business income tax paid is multiplied by 55%, encoded as 0.55. Line 5 multiplies net foreign business income divided by net income by basic federal tax. Line 6 is the federal surtax on income allocated to Other Outside Canada: either the amount from line 10 of Part 2 of Form T2203 or 48% of line 42900, encoded as 0.48. Line 7 adds line 5 and line 6. Line 8 adds basic federal tax and line 6. Line 9 is the amount from line 3, if any. Line 10 subtracts line 9 from line 8. Line 11 is the lesser of line 4, line 7, or line 10. Line 12 is the amount from line 3, if any. Line 13 adds line 11 and line 12, should not be more than line 42900 plus any applicable federal surtax on income earned outside Canada, and is entered on line 40500 of the return.
